accurate.why: All claims match the bill text—added organizations, podiatric medicine integration, schools of pharmacy, and retroactive effective date are correctly described.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; all listed organizations appear in the bill text and amendment is to the Controlled Substances Act as stated.

states_purpose.why: Summary clearly states the bill clarifies and expands training requirements for prescribers of controlled substances.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Summary correctly identifies the amendment of Section 303 of the Controlled Substances Act and the changes to recognized organizations and pathways.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a list-expansion amendment with no material exceptions or carve-outs to note.

effective_dates.why: Summary notes the retroactive effective date but is cut off mid-sentence before stating it takes effect as if enacted December 29, 2022.

major_provisions_covered.why: Summary captures all essential operative effects: added organizations, podiatric medicine inclusion, schools of pharmacy, and retroactive effective date.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Summary is well-organized and clear, though it ends abruptly mid-sentence on the effective date.

concise.why: Summary is focused and free of padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: All named organizations and the Controlled Substances Act/Section 303 are correctly identified per the bill text.

correct_figures.why: The summary states no specific figures incorrectly; the effective date sentence is truncated before any date is stated.
